freeze.hashRule: protocol.json is authenticated by the SHA-256 of its parsed JSON with ONLY the nested `freeze.hashes` map removed and remaining keys recursively sorted, then serialized compactly — self-authenticating (baselineCommit/sealedStage/hashAlgorithm/hashRule/note are all covered) and still non-circular (the hashes map that stores this value is the only thing excluded). fences.json and every baseline/* file are hashed over exact on-disk bytes. TWO-COMMIT topology: freeze.baselineCommit points at the functional commit A that carries these exact non-freeze protocol payload + fences.json + baseline/* bytes; this freeze-only commit B changes solely protocol.json.freeze. `npm run freeze-gate` recomputes the integrity hashes AND proves commit A contains the same bytes (whole freeze block stripped) as HEAD.
freeze.note: A non-null baselineCommit means the baseline IS sealed: commit A is present and an ancestor of HEAD, the frozen files match the integrity hashes above, and A's protocol payload (freeze stripped) + fences.json + baseline/* are byte-identical to HEAD. A null baselineCommit means NOT sealed. freeze-gate fails closed on a null/missing/inappropriate commit, a hash mismatch, a missing/extra frozen file, or an A-vs-B content mismatch.

sessions.epochRule: Any product, editor, tool, adapter, or runtime change after the first scored session invalidates the entire epoch; fresh matched briefs restart both lanes.
